docs.text: garmin-dayly Fill Google Sheets with fitness data from Garmin Connect. Installation pip install garmin-daily Credentials Garmin Connect Place login and password into env vars GARMIN_EMAIL and GARMIN_PASSWORD respectfully export GARMIN_EMAIL="andrey@sorokin.engineer" export GARMIN_PASSWORD='password' Google Sheets Get Google credentials for Google Sheet as explained in gspread:Using Service Account Place it to ~/.config/gspread/service_account.json . Do not forget to grant access to you sheets for this service emails. Google Sheet structure Expected columns: Location Sport Duration Date Distance Steps Comment Week Hours Week Day HR rest Sleep time VO2 max First row should be with the columns' titles. You can add another column titles in the mapping COLUMNS_MAP garmin-daily command line interface To add rows to Google Sheet from Garmin Connect, use garmin-daily , get help with garmin-daily --help At minimum, you specify Google Sheet name and that's all garmin-daily --sheet "My Fitness" It also can automatically create you gym trainings if you have them on specific days. It's easier to correct already created rows then to create them manually from scratch. List the week days with gym trainings in the parameters and specify your gym location and usual training duration: garmin-daily --sheet "My Fitness" \ -g mon -g tue -g fri \ --gym-duration 30 \ --gym-location "Cool place" GitHUB sorokin.engineer/garmin-daily
docs.title: garmin-dayly

docs.text: To add rows to Google Sheet from Garmin Connect, use garmin-daily , get help with garmin-daily --help At minimum, you specify Google Sheet name and that's all garmin-daily --sheet "My Fitness" It also can automatically create you gym trainings if you have them on specific days. It's easier to correct already created rows then to create them manually from scratch. List the week days with gym trainings in the parameters and specify your gym location and usual training duration: garmin-daily --sheet "My Fitness" \ -g mon -g tue -g fri \ --gym-duration 30 \ --gym-location "Cool place"

docs.text: API Overview Modules columns_mapper : Map fields to columns using spreadsheet header row. garmin_aggregations : Garmin data aggregated daily. google_sheet : Export Garmin data to Google Sheet. snake_to_camel : Convert names from Python (snake-name) to Java (camelName) name convention and vice-versa. version Classes columns_mapper.ColumnsMapper : Map columns based on a spreadsheet header row. columns_mapper.GarminCol : Columns of the Garmin daily data spreadsheet. garmin_aggregations.Activity : Garmin activity. garmin_aggregations.ActivityField : Activity field description. garmin_aggregations.AggFunc : Activity field aggregation function. garmin_aggregations.GarminDaily : Aggregate activities daily. garmin_aggregations.GarminDay : Aggregate one day Garmin data. google_sheet.Weekdays : Weekdays for datetime.weekday. Functions google_sheet.add_rows_from_garmin : Add activities from Garmin to the Google Sheet. google_sheet.create_day_rows : Sheet rows for the day. google_sheet.detect_days_to_add : Get last filled date and calculate number of days to add till today. google_sheet.localized_csv_raw : Convert fields to the Google Sheet locale specific string representation. google_sheet.open_google_sheet : Open Google Sheet. google_sheet.search_missed_steps_in_sheet : Add missed in Garmin API steps data from earlier entered in the spreadsheet. google_sheet.sheet_week_day : Weekday number for Google Sheet. google_sheet.week_num : Week number for the sheet (starting from some arbitrary date). snake_to_camel.camel_to_upper_snake : Convert camelCase to UPPER_SNAKE_CASE. snake_to_camel.snake_to_camel : Convert snake_case_names to camelCase. This file was automatically generated via lazydocs .

docs.text: module google_sheet Export Garmin data to Google Sheet. Global Variables SPORT_STEP_LENGTH_KM WALKING_SPORT VERSION SHEET_NAME_DEFAULT PCWeekdays BATCH_SIZE API_DELAY DAY_TO_ADD_WITHOUT_FORCE function sheet_week_day sheet_week_day(day: date) → int Weekday number for Google Sheet. Monday - 1 ... Sunday - 7 function week_num week_num(day: date) → int Week number for the sheet (starting from some arbitrary date). function add_rows_from_garmin add_rows_from_garmin( fitness: Worksheet, columns: ColumnsMapper, start_date: date, days_to_add: int, gym_days: List[int], gym_duration: int, gym_location: str ) → None Add activities from Garmin to the Google Sheet. function search_missed_steps_in_sheet search_missed_steps_in_sheet( fitness: Worksheet, rows: List[List[str]], columns: ColumnsMapper ) → None Add missed in Garmin API steps data from earlier entered in the spreadsheet. We need that only if we need very old data - for some reason Garmin API do not return steps more than for last three months. May be we can use some flag to get them - in the Garmin App you can see this steps but not from the API. Anyway in such situation if we already had entered steps manually in the Google Sheet we can find and use them. Just once I needed this mode so I keep it in the code. If you have steps from Garmin API we won't read the Google Sheet to Pandas DataFrame so this code won't take any resources. function detect_days_to_add detect_days_to_add( fitness: Worksheet, columns: ColumnsMapper ) → Tuple[date, int] Get last filled date and calculate number of days to add till today. Returns (start_date, days_to_add) function open_google_sheet open_google_sheet(sheet: str) → Tuple[Worksheet, ColumnsMapper] Open Google Sheet. Return worksheet and columns map. Also set current locale from the spreadsheet so it will affect %n formatting. function create_day_rows create_day_rows( daily: GarminDaily, day: date, gym_duration: int, gym_days: List[int], gym_location: str ) → List[Dict[GarminCol, Union[str, int, float, NoneType]]] Sheet rows for the day. function localized_csv_raw localized_csv_raw(row: List[Optional[str, int, float]]) → List[str] Convert fields to the Google Sheet locale specific string representation. class Weekdays Weekdays for datetime.weekday. This file was automatically generated via lazydocs .

docs.text: add_rows_from_garmin( fitness: Worksheet, columns: ColumnsMapper, start_date: date, days_to_add: int, gym_days: List[int], gym_duration: int, gym_location: str ) → None Add activities from Garmin to the Google Sheet.
docs.title: function add_rows_from_garmin

docs.text: search_missed_steps_in_sheet( fitness: Worksheet, rows: List[List[str]], columns: ColumnsMapper ) → None Add missed in Garmin API steps data from earlier entered in the spreadsheet. We need that only if we need very old data - for some reason Garmin API do not return steps more than for last three months. May be we can use some flag to get them - in the Garmin App you can see this steps but not from the API. Anyway in such situation if we already had entered steps manually in the Google Sheet we can find and use them. Just once I needed this mode so I keep it in the code. If you have steps from Garmin API we won't read the Google Sheet to Pandas DataFrame so this code won't take any resources.
docs.title: function search_missed_steps_in_sheet

docs.text: detect_days_to_add( fitness: Worksheet, columns: ColumnsMapper ) → Tuple[date, int] Get last filled date and calculate number of days to add till today. Returns (start_date, days_to_add)
docs.title: function detect_days_to_add

docs.text: open_google_sheet(sheet: str) → Tuple[Worksheet, ColumnsMapper] Open Google Sheet. Return worksheet and columns map. Also set current locale from the spreadsheet so it will affect %n formatting.
docs.title: function open_google_sheet
